name: Pork St. Tammany
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook rice according to package directions. Set aside.
2. Pour boiling water over apricots; let stand 20 minutes to soften. Drain.
3. In a large skillet, sauté green onions, mushrooms, and green pepper in butter until tender.
4. Stir in rice, apricots, pecans, parsley, and seasonings.
5. Remove strings from pork loin roast and slice it lengthwise into half pieces.
6. Slice the pork pieces lengthwise again, but not all the way through on one side.
7. Lay the pork pieces side by side and spoon the stuffing mixture evenly over them.
8. Begin with one long side of the pork; roll it tightly, like a jellyroll, and tie securely at 2-inch intervals.
9. Place the roast in a lightly greased roasting pan on a rack.
10. Arrange bacon lengthwise over the roast.
11. Insert a meat thermometer into the thickest part of the roast, ensuring it does not touch fat or stuffing.
12. Cover with aluminum foil and bake at 325°F for 3 hours or until the meat thermometer reads 160°F (30-35 minutes per pound).
13. Remove the foil for the last 30-40 minutes of baking.
14. Let the roast stand for 5 minutes before slicing.
15. Slice and garnish with apricot halves and parsley, if desired.
Yield: 12 to 15 servings.
